android: fix duplicate method names in UsbSerial.java

Java does not allow static and instance methods with the same signature.
Renamed instance methods to openImpl/closeImpl/writeImpl/readImpl so
the static JNI wrappers (open/close/write/read) compile cleanly.

commit.message:
feat: edge case hardening for all commands (issue #16) (#26)

* refactor: remove WorldBuilder in favour of JSON world loader

WorldBuilder.Build() was a dead code path -- Program.cs always loads
neon-ledger.json when the file exists, and the JSON is committed to
the repo. Replacing the if/else with an explicit FileNotFoundException
makes the missing-file case visible instead of silently loading a
partial fallback world.

Updated GameWorldTests and GameIntegrationTests to load the world from
neon-ledger.json via JsonWorldLoader. Tests now reflect the JSON world
map (bar->east->plaza, lobby->west->corridor, full winning path through
checkpoint with cred_chip). All 168 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: drone threat lose condition (issue #8) (#24)

* squad: Record orchestration—Judy remove WorldBuilder, test count 168

- Orchestration logs: Judy (WorldBuilder removal) and Coordinator (PR #22 closed)
- Session log: Remove WorldBuilder migration summary
- Decisions: Merged WorldBuilder removal decision from inbox
- Judy history: Session 7 entry with test count update (168 passing)
- Inbox cleanup: Deleted judy-remove-worldbuilder.md from decisions/inbox

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: implement drone threat lose condition (issue #8)

- Add DroneThreatLevel, HasLost, HighRiskRoomIds to GameState
- Increment threat counter each turn spent in plaza/checkpoint
- Trigger lose sequence after 4 turns in high-risk zones (threshold raised
  from spec's 3 to 4 so the winning path — max 2 turns per room — remains
  viable; River flagged this in DroneTests.cs WinningPath regression test)
- Print escalating warning messages at turns 1, 2, and 3
- Add LoseMessage/HighRiskRoomIds to LoadedWorld and JSON loader
- Add lose text and highRiskRoomIds to neon-ledger.json
- Update DroneTests.cs: fix xUnit assertion syntax, align thresholds to 4

Closes #8

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Judy <judy@mygame.squad>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: edge case hardening for all commands (issue #16)

- SaveCommand/LoadCommand: strip directory components to prevent path traversal
- LoadCommand: null-safe iteration over Inventory/Flags; nullable SaveData properties; validate CurrentRoomId not null
- GoCommand: validate TargetRoomId exists before moving to prevent KeyNotFoundException
- TakeCommand/DropCommand: use ColorConsole.Error for consistent error message styling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: edge case tests for command hardening (issue #16)

22 tests covering: GoCommand invalid target room, locked exits, null direction;
SaveCommand path traversal, null noun, extension handling; LoadCommand file-not-
found, corrupted JSON, null inventory/flags, path traversal, unknown room/item;
TakeCommand and DropCommand null nouns and missing items; CommandRegistry unknown
verb.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor for readability, add diagrams and white paper

Major refactoring following programming principles (readability first,
small functions, DRY, meaningful names, no scripts):

Code changes:
- design.py: Break 220-line recurrent_mlp() into 10 focused functions
  (_validate_config, _define_kernels, _create_direct_fifos,
   _create_memtile_fifos, _make_worker_body, _column_activation_tap,
   _weight_tap, _create_runtime_sequence, etc.)
- test.py: Replace hardcoded paths with IRON_DIR env var + Path discovery;
  extract generate_test_data(), run_npu(), run_cpu(), check_correctness(),
  compute_metrics(), print_results() from monolithic benchmark()
- op.py: Add class/method docstrings, extract _artifact_name(), use class
  constants for compiler flags
- __init__.py: Improve module docstring, add assertion messages, rename
  block_r/block_c to block_rows/block_cols
- mlp_kernels.cc: Document SIMD width, alignment requirements, kernel
  contracts; rename size/count to num_elements

Documentation:
- docs/generate_figures.py: Creates 3 high-quality matplotlib diagrams
  (hardware architecture, recurrent MLP dataflow, performance scaling)
- docs/generate_whitepaper.py: Generates PDF white paper via weasyprint
  combining architecture explanation, diagrams, and benchmark results
- README.md: Replace ASCII art with diagram images, add project structure
  section, link to white paper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES support for Mock Cluster (#2099)

### Issue

#2070 

### Description

Add support for TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES when creating mock and simulation
clusters as well. So far, target_devices were taking just the chips
specified in that set, not the ones from the boards where target chips
are. After this PR, user can use both TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES and
target_device_ids for creating mock and simulation cluster. When all
users transition to TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES, we could remove the support for
that.

### List of the changes

- Support TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES for mock and simulation cluster
- Refactor logic around creating cluster descriptor
- Take the chips from the same board where target devices are

### Testing
CI

### API Changes
/

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Ridvan Song <56486612+Riddy21@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Riddy21 <rsong@g14glx03.tenstorrent.net>

commit.message:
fix: use statusRef in search debounce to avoid stale closure

The search debounce captured status from its mount-time closure,
so if the user changed the status filter within 300ms of page load
the debounce would fire with the stale 'all' value, overwriting the
correct filtered results. Fix by mirroring the existing searchRef
pattern for status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: outfit archetypes, one-piece/coord-set support, new enums & fields

## Summary
Implements the full archetype-based outfit system as specified:

### Part 1 — app_enums.dart
- Add `ClothingCategory.onePiece` ('One Piece')
- Add `ClothingSubcategory` enum (tops, bottoms, one-piece, shoes, outerwear)
- Add `ShoeFormality` enum (formal, casual, sporty)
- Add `OutfitArchetype` enum (separates, onePiece, onePieceLayered, coordSet, smartCasual)

### Part 2 — clothing_item_model.dart
- Add `subcategory`, `shoeFormality`, `setId`, `isOnePiece`, `replacesTop`,
  `replacesBottom` fields with safe defaults (backward-compatible)

### Part 3 — app_database.dart + app_constants.dart
- Bump schema version 2 → 3
- Add migration for new ClothingItems columns and Outfits.archetype/onePieceId

### Part 4 — archetype_engine.dart (NEW)
- `OutfitCombination`, `CoordSet`, `ArchetypeEngine`
- Detects available archetypes; generates season-filtered combinations

### Part 5 — outfit_scoring_engine.dart
- Delegates to `ArchetypeEngine`; adds shoe-pairing bonus and coord-set bonus
- Existing totalCombinationsEvaluated formula preserved (backward compat)

### Part 6 — outfit_model.dart
- Add `onePieceId` (nullable String) and `archetype` (OutfitArchetype)

### Part 7 — tagging_form.dart + add_item_providers.dart
- Dynamic form: subcategory selector, shoe formality, co-ord set section
- `TaggingFormState` extended with new fields; `isValid` updated

### Part 8 — clothing_item_card.dart
- Visual badges: ONE PIECE overlay, Co-ord chip, shoe formality chip

### Part 9 — filter_chip_bar.dart
- One Piece filter chip in correct position (Top, Bottom, One Piece, Shoes, Outerwear)

### Part 10 — outfit_card.dart
- Archetype label chip; coord-set link indicator between paired items

### Part 11 — add_item_usecase.dart
- `AddItemInput` extended with subcategory/shoeFormality/setId/isCoordPiece

### Part 12 — local_clothing_datasource.dart + clothing_repository.dart
- New methods: `getOnePieceItems`, `getItemsBySetId`, `getCoordSets`,
  `linkCoordSet`, `unlinkCoordSet`
- Row mapping updated for all new columns

### Part 13 — Tests
- `home_screen_test.dart`: fix saveAll stub, add todaysOutfitsProvider override,
  update icon/text to match current home_screen implementation
- `archetype_engine_test.dart` (NEW): 19 tests covering archetype detection,
  combination generation, scoring bonuses, and backward compat
- `repository_providers.dart`: remove unnecessary LocalOutfitDatasource cast
  in todaysOutfitsProvider (was breaking test setup)

All 121 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cfl: reconcile patches against upstream v2.3.1.5 (61 patches, 0 failures)

- Regenerated CFL-033 (TiffImg malloc overflow) for upstream #659 context shift
- Merged CFL-047 (SequenceNeedTempReset depth) into CFL-074 (combined patch)
- CFL-074 now protects both CheckUnderflowOverflow AND SequenceNeedTempReset
  with ops budget + recursion depth limits (CWE-400/CWE-674)
- Deleted CFL-047 (functionality merged into CFL-074)

commit.message:
fix: remove Docker ARG for NEXT_PUBLIC_* — use .env.production as sole source

Root cause: Docker ARG values (even empty strings) are visible as env vars
during RUN, overriding .env.production. No amount of conditional export/unset
fixes this reliably.

治本: .env.production is the single source of truth for NEXT_PUBLIC_* values.
Removed all ARG/ENV/--build-arg machinery from Dockerfile, cloudbuild.yaml,
and deploy-gcp.yml.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update frontend/src/features/memories/constants.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: migrate from @variantjs/vue to @nuxt/ui (#2919)

### 🔗 Linked issue

### 📚 Description

Removes `@variantjs/vue` and `@variantjs/core` entirely, replacing all
usages with equivalent Nuxt UI components.

**Component replacements:**
- `t-alert` → `UAlert`
- `t-checkbox` → `UCheckbox`
- `t-dropdown` → `UDropdownMenu`
- `t-input` → `UInput`
- `t-input-group` → `UFormField`
- `t-select` → `USelect`
- `t-tag` → `UBadge`
- `t-textarea` → `UTextarea`

**Files changed:**
- `plugins/tailwind.ts` — removed all variantjs config/registrations;
retains only the custom `TTable.vue` registration
- `components/*.stories.vue` — story names preserved; components under
test swapped to Nuxt UI equivalents
- `components/NavBar.vue`, `DocumentEditor.vue`,
`DocumentEditorInput.vue`, `DocumentView.vue`, `PasswordInput.vue` —
inline usages replaced
- `pages/user/register.vue`, `forgot-password.vue`,
`change-password/[token].vue` — form field/alert patterns migrated
- `app.config.ts` — updated Nuxt UI color mappings from custom semantic
names (`'error'`, `'success'`, etc.) to actual Tailwind palette names
(`'red'`, `'green'`, etc.) to restore full color shade generation

> `t-nuxtlink` (custom `defineNuxtLink`) and `t-table` (custom
`TTable.vue`) were not variantjs components and are unchanged.

**Color fix:** The old `plugins/tailwind.ts` contained CSS class strings
(e.g. `bg-error-50`, `text-error-700`) as part of the variantjs
configuration, which caused Tailwind v4 to generate all color utility
classes and their corresponding CSS variables. Removing this file caused
only shades 300 and 600 to be generated for semantic colors, breaking
Nuxt UI component colors. The fix maps semantic colors to actual
Tailwind palette names (`error → red`, `success → green`, `warning →
amber`, `secondary → rose`, `info → sky`) so that `--ui-color-*`
variables always resolve to the fully-included standard Tailwind
palette.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

pull requests. You can protect all pull requests by enabling Advanced
Security for your repositories. [Learn more about Advanced
Security.](https://gh.io/cca-advanced-security)

---------

feat: FastAPI gateway + Streamlit dashboard

hospital-api (SOLID architecture):
- main.py: app factory with MLflow UC config and router registration
- core/config.py: Pydantic Settings for all env vars
- core/security.py: JWT creation and verification
- schemas/requests.py: focused Pydantic request models
- services/base.py: abstract PredictionService (O/L principle)
- services/model_registry.py: MLflow model loading singleton + SHAP explainer
- services/forecast_service.py: Pipeline 1 - Prophet admission forecasting (FHIR R4)
- services/staffing_service.py: Pipeline 2 - LP optimized staff schedule
- services/alert_service.py: Pipeline 3 - XGBoost risk with SHAP top-8 explanation
- routers/: auth, health, forecast, staffing, alerts endpoints
- requirements.txt: pinned working deps (fastapi, mlflow, pandas, xgboost, shap)
- Dockerfile: production container

hospital-dashboard (Streamlit):
- app.py: login + role-based sidebar navigation
- api_client.py: centralised HTTP client for all API calls
- views/doctor.py: 5 sample patients, live risk scores, SHAP bar chart, alert slider
- views/manager.py: Gantt timeline, coverage heatmap, stacked shift bars, KPIs
- views/executive.py: forecast line+CI, KPI cards, model health, all-pipeline KPIs

commit.message:
Remove Break Music, Song Shop, and related UI elements from mainwindow.ui

Removed:
- Break Music tab (tab_2) with all child widgets (splitterBm, tableViewBmDb,
  tableViewBmPlaylist, comboBoxBmPlaylists, all Bm labels/buttons/sliders,
  playlist reorder buttons btnPlTop/Up/Down/Bottom, btnBmPlRandomize)
- Song Shop button (pushButtonShop) from the main karaoke tab
- videoPreviewBm widget from the video preview area
- menuBreak_Music QMenu and its submenu menuPlaylists
- actionSong_Shop from the Karaoke menu (with separator)
- actionBreak_music_torture from the Testing menu
- addaction reference to menuBreak_Music from the menu bar
- All related action definitions: actionManage_Break_DB, actionDisplay_Metadata,
  actionDisplay_Filenames, actionPlaylistNew/Import/Export/Delete,
  actionSong_Shop, actionBreak_music_torture

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: strip "+" prefix from git branch output in orphan detection

git branch marks branches checked out in other worktrees with a "+"
prefix. Only "*" (current branch) was being stripped, causing worktree
branches to appear as false orphans and triggering double deletion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: frontend Dockerfile ENV overriding .env.production with empty values

Empty ARG → ENV set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL to empty string,
overriding .env.production values. Now only exports ARG when non-empty,
letting Next.js naturally read .env.production.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

cfl: regenerate patches 079-081, delete 6 NO-OPs, harden build.sh

Regenerated 3 malformed patches that never applied:
- CFL-079: ApplySequence runtime depth limit (16) — CWE-674
- CFL-080: XYZ Describe output size cap (1MB) — CWE-400
- CFL-081: DescribeSequence recursion depth limit (32) — CWE-674

CFL-079 now includes both .h declaration changes (ApplySequence +
DescribeSequence signatures) and .cpp ApplySequence implementation.
CFL-081 has only .cpp DescribeSequence hunks (header handled by 079).

Deleted 6 NO-OP patches (upstreamed or superseded):
- 006 (upstreamed), 043 (superseded by 047), 048 (superseded by 074)
- 064 (upstreamed PR #652), 070 (upstreamed PR #654), 072 (upstreamed PR #657)

Hardened build.sh patch loop:
- Distinguish 'already applied' (reverse dry-run) from 'FAIL' (malformed)
- Report summary: N applied, N skipped, N failed
- Warn when failures detected (CWE-400 protections may be missing)

Verified:
- 62 patches, 0 failures, 18 fuzzers built
- All 8 timeout artifacts complete instantly (<100ms)
- 230/230 unit tests pass
- Fuzzers synced to SSD at /mnt/g/fuzz-ssd/bin/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(types): make templateParams extensible via module augmentation (#679)

* fix(types): make templateParams extensible via module augmentation

Add `templateParams` to `SchemaAugmentations` and intersect it into
`ResolvableTemplateParams`, allowing users to extend templateParams
via `declare module 'unhead/types' { interface SchemaAugmentations { templateParams: { foo: string } } }`.
Also updates `ReactiveHead.templateParams` in the Vue package to use
the shared `ResolvableTemplateParams` type.

Closes #575

* fix(types): make templateParams extensible via module augmentation (#684)

* Initial plan

* fix(types): use dedicated TemplateParamsAugmentations interface to avoid TS2717

Co-authored-by: harlan-zw <5326365+harlan-zw@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: harlan-zw <5326365+harlan-zw@users.noreply.github.com>
